content.full_text:
OTFICE OF THE
CITY ATTORNEY
196 E. Fitth Strs€t
PO. Box 3,120
Chlco, CA 95927
(916) 89s-4826
[ro I .l le Pll '91
february 4, 19 91
Ca,/PEND/20:34-7
Eair Political Practices Cornmission
428 "J" Stleet, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 
95 814
Attention! Legal Assistance Division
This is a request for an opinion on the propriety of a city
councilmember participating 
in 
council 
deliberations
concerning action requested by a large real estate developer,
if that councilmember is a vice-president of a bank which
hol.ds deposit accounts for 
that 
developer and the
councilmember supervises those accounts. The councilmember, s
nanne is ltark Francis and he has authorized this request.
SpecificalLy, the facts are as follows: The councilmember is
employed by Bank of America as a vice-president. 
The
developer in question is engaged in real property development
in the city of Chico. 
The developer maintains deposit
accounts at Bank of America but does not have any loans from
Bank of America. In the course of the councilmember, s
employment, he acts as the banker for the developer.
Pursuant to that relationship, the councilmember is the
person at Bank of Anerica who is contacted by the developer
in the event the developer has questions or concerns
regarding his banking needs or accounts and the councilmember
offers advice as to holi those banking needs may be met.
In the event that any of the developer,s deposit accounts
were to become overdrawn in an amount up to $501000.00, the
councilmember would be the person responsible for rnaking a
decision whether or not to make a loan to the developer in
order to cover the overdraft. In the event the accounts were
to become overdrawn in an amount greate! than g50,000.00, the
same decision would be made by the counciLmember and one
other vice president at the bank.
Bank of America is a Fortune 500 company and it can be
assumed that the existence or loss of the amounts held in the
deposit accounts in question rrould not be sufficient to have
a material financial effect on the Bank. In addition, the
existence or loss of those accounts can be assumed to have no
effect on the councilmember, s source of income.
Since the developer engages in a substantial amount of
property development within the City, it is very 1ike1y that


the city council will be called upon in the future to act on
various development approvals at the request of the
developer, such as general plan amendments, specific plans or
rezones .
The specific issue which I would like you to address is
whether the councilmember's economic interest would be
directly involved in such a decision, pursuant to section
18702.1(a) (1) of Title 
2 of the California 
Code of
Reg"ulations, based upon the existence of a nexus between the
purpose for which the councilmember receives income and the
governmental decision.
Thank you for your assistance in this matter.
Very trul,y yours ,
Robert G. Boehm
City Attorney
cc: City I'lanager
City Cl-erk
Counc ilmember llark Francis
Bar

sections.question: whether the councilmember's economic interest would be directly involved in such a decision, pursuant to section 18702.1(a)(1) of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations, based upon the existence of a nexus between the purpose for which the councilmember receives income and the governmental decision.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city councilmember who is a Bank of America vice-president supervising deposit accounts for a real estate developer has a disqualifying economic interest that would prevent him from participating in council decisions regarding that developer's requests for development approvals.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section exists. Document is from City Attorney Robert G. Boehm requesting FPPC opinion on conflict of interest question. Document ID format suggests this may be UNK (Unknown) type, likely pending FPPC response.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the City Attorney of Chico asking the FPPC whether Councilmember Mark Francis has a disqualifying economic interest under California law by virtue of his employment as a vice-president at Bank of America while supervising deposit accounts for a real estate developer who regularly seeks council approvals.
